rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BORG ET AL VS. 355 FULTON STREET, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 24, 2020 line 1. Mary Catherine Wiederhold, esq.'s MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD for PLAINTIFF R. LEE JEWELL READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Department501ContestTR@sftc.org e-mail address shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is GRANTED. Counsel to prepare order. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL PLATT ET AL VS. BILL QUAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 24, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANTs BILL QUAN, LAI KING QUAN PROPERTIES, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Judgment READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Department501ContestTR@sftc.org shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendants did not establish a complete statute of limitations defense to Plaintiffs' causes of action. Defendants also failed to shift the burden to Plaintiffs regarding a reasonable time to vacate the premises for Plaintiffs' ninth cause of action for constructive eviction. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). Defendants' Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendants did not demonstrate that there is no merit to Plaintiffs' claims. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NEIL PENAFLOR ET AL VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 24, 2020 line 3. Megan Johnson, Wolford Wayne LLP's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD for PLAINTIFFs NEIL PENAFLOR, BIENVENIDA PENAFLOR, and CHARLIE PENAFLOR READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Department501ContestTR@sftc.org e-mail address shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is DENIED. No reason for withdrawal provided. " An attorney may not, in the absence of the client's consent, withdraw from a case without justifiable cause" Lovvorn v. Johnston (9th Cir. 1941) 118 F.2d 704, 706 = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL S NOUNOU VS. ANDREW LESKO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement By Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 24, 2020 line 12. DEFENDANTs ANDREW LESKO and TARA JUELL's Motion To Enforce Settlement By Judgment READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Department501ContestTR@sftc.org e-mail address shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Enforce Settlement by Judgement is OFF CALENDAR. This motion was filed during stay per April 30, 2020 General Order of the Presiding Judge. No order lifting the stay was sought and no required determination of necessity to proceed with this action during the stay was made. The first court day after the stay has been lifted was June 22, 2020, only 2 court days before the hearing date set by the moving party. The Court notes that based on the papers submitted in support of and in opposition to this motion, the parties have not completed their negotiations regarding formal settlement agreement. If parties decide to re-file this motion on proper notice, further good faith attempts to comply with the mutual agreement to enter into a formal settlement shall be made. = (501/CFH)
